title: Conagra Brands · Production Line Scheduling
subtitle: MIT Sloan Capstone · Data Science Capstone Intern
highlights: Building a production-line scheduling tool that helps plant managers minimize equipment downtime and changeover costs
Establishing a scalable blueprint for company-wide data standardization across Databricks, SAP, and Power BI
Integrating an agentic AI solution for responding to unexpected events, enabling real-time schedule adjustments
description: MIT Sloan Master of Business Analytics capstone with Conagra Brands as a Data Science Capstone Intern. The project builds an optimization-driven scheduling tool for production lines, paired with an agentic AI layer for real-time disruption response, and lays the groundwork for company-wide data standardization. Detailed materials are confidential under the program's industry charter, so this entry stays at a high level.

title: Optimization · Water Access in the DRC
subtitle: MIT Sloan · Optimization Methods
highlights: Engineered scalable optimization pipelines in Julia / JuMP
Identified high-impact well locations across the Democratic Republic of Congo that maximize population reach and minimize travel distance
Improved projected access to water by 19%
description: MIT Sloan Optimization Methods project applying mixed-integer programming to humanitarian infrastructure planning. The model selects new well locations across the Democratic Republic of Congo to maximize population reached and minimize travel distance to clean water, subject to budget and geographic constraints. Pipelines were built to scale across very large candidate-site sets and to compare multiple policy scenarios.

title: Predictive + Prescriptive Solar Battery Strategy
subtitle: MIT Sloan · Machine Learning
highlights: Designed an optimized schedule in Julia to prescribe battery charging, holding, and selling strategies
Forecasted next-day solar energy availability and electricity market prices
Increased projected profits by 20%
description: MIT Sloan Machine Learning project that combines predictive modeling and prescriptive optimization to operate a grid-scale battery. The predictive layer forecasts next-day solar generation and market prices; the prescriptive layer is an optimization in Julia that decides charging, holding, and selling actions to maximize revenue subject to physical battery constraints. The full pipeline demonstrates how ML and optimization can be cleanly chained together end-to-end.

title: Boston Public Health Commission · Shelter Occupancy Forecasting
subtitle: MIT Sloan · Analytics Lab · ML Project Lead
highlights: Deployed Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and ARIMA models to forecast 14-day homeless shelter occupancy
Integrated outputs in a Streamlit application, improving capacity planning accuracy by 50%
Identified key social, environmental, and temporal drivers of shelter utilization through feature engineering and interpretability analysis
Selected for poster presentation at the 2026 CSTE Annual Conference (Boston, MA), reaching 2,800+ public health professionals
description: MIT Sloan Analytics Lab project with the Boston Public Health Commission. The team built and deployed a suite of machine-learning models — Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and ARIMA — to forecast 14-day occupancy across the city's homeless shelter network, with results integrated into a Streamlit application for program managers. Feature-importance and interpretability analysis surfaced the social, environmental, and temporal factors driving demand, enabling better resource allocation. The work was selected for poster presentation at the 2026 CSTE Annual Conference.

title: Greater Boston · Data Visualization Proof of Concept
subtitle: MIT · Data Visualization team project
highlights: Interactive proof-of-concept visualization of Greater Boston
Live deployment hosted on GitHub Pages
Collaborative MIT team project focused on cartographic and chart design
description: Collaborative MIT data-visualization project exploring Greater Boston through an interactive proof-of-concept. The project showcases how careful chart design and geospatial layering can make a complex regional dataset legible and explorable. View the live site for the full experience.

title: Optimization · F1 Race Calendar Redesign
subtitle: UC Berkeley · Nonlinear & Discrete Optimization
highlights: Modeled the Formula 1 calendar as a routing / sequencing optimization in AMPL
Re-sequenced races to minimize travel distance while maintaining venue requirements
Cut average emissions by an estimated 927 tons of carbon dioxide per team
description: UC Berkeley Nonlinear & Discrete Optimization project that re-imagines the Formula 1 calendar as a constrained routing problem. The AMPL model finds a re-ordering of races that minimizes global travel distance while respecting venue and seasonal requirements, reducing average emissions by an estimated 927 tons of CO₂ per team.

title: Voltage Discharge Prediction · Hydrogen Electrolyzers
subtitle: Verdagy · Data Science Intern
highlights: Automated Python algorithms to clean real-time raw sensor data, removing noise, outliers, and sensor malfunctions
Reduced data cleaning labor by 80% and improved summary-statistic accuracy
Designed and rolled out 9 dashboards in Python and Seeq to track voltage drift and hydrogen production for 10 lab-scale electrolyzers
description: Industry research at Verdagy on alkaline electrolyzer performance. Built Python pipelines to ingest, denoise, and align multivariate sensor streams from 10 lab-scale electrolyzers, then designed nine dashboards in Python and Seeq for real-time monitoring of voltage drift and hydrogen production — enabling faster R&D decision-making for scientists and executives.

title: Yelp Sentiment Analysis
subtitle: UC Berkeley · NLP project
highlights: Tokenized and modeled millions of Yelp reviews
Quantified sentiment patterns across business categories
Surfaced category-level satisfaction signal for downstream analysis
description: UC Berkeley NLP project applying text preprocessing, tokenization, and sentiment classification to the public Yelp Open Dataset. The work measures customer satisfaction at the business and category level and explores the linguistic features most predictive of star ratings.

title: MongoDB vs SQL · Query Performance Analysis
subtitle: UC Berkeley · Data Engineering
highlights: Benchmarked equivalent analytical queries across MongoDB and SQL
Compared query speed, scalability, and developer ergonomics
Wrote up recommendations for when to choose each store
description: Data-engineering project that puts MongoDB and a relational SQL store head-to-head on the Yelp dataset. Equivalent analytical queries were authored in both systems and benchmarked across speed, scalability, and ease of expression, informing when each technology is the right tool.

title: Product Analytics · Marketing Kaggle Competition
subtitle: MIT Sloan · Product Analytics · Top 10 leaderboard finish
highlights: Top-10 leaderboard finish in a class-wide marketing-analytics Kaggle competition
Heavy feature engineering and ensembling on customer-behavior data
Demonstrates practical product-analytics modeling end-to-end
description: Kaggle-style marketing analytics competition where my team placed in the top 10 on the leaderboard. The project involved heavy feature engineering on customer-behavior data and ensembling of gradient-boosted models, included here as evidence of practical product-analytics work.

title: ArcGIS Mapping · ESPM 110
subtitle: UC Berkeley · Environmental health spatial analyses
highlights: Produced map deliverables for UC Berkeley's ESPM 110
Layered ecological and demographic data for environmental analysis
Practiced classical cartographic design for technical reports
description: Selected map outputs from coursework in UC Berkeley's ESPM 110 (The Politics & Ecology of Environmental Health). The full reports are coursework, but the map deliverables — included on the dedicated Maps page — showcase hands-on ArcGIS work: data layering, spatial joins, classification, and cartographic design.
